gaps.description: Bill of Rights is a scaffold awaiting human authorship. The MSP requires a human-authored BoR to define the enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, and amendment protocol that the operational constitution must enforce. Without it, the DIO operates with an undefined security perimeter and no authoritative foundation for Nomos evaluation.

Governance organs (Senate, Arbiter, Nomos) are unstable, flipping between DEGRADED and ALIVE every 30 seconds, as shown in Spine state transitions. The MSP amendment pipeline relies on Senate-Legislator responding to PEMs, but an unstable Senate cannot reliably process policy exceptions or amend the MSP. Nomos enforcement and Arbiter scope checks may be intermittent.

Mission Statement Protocol contains no rules; the organism operates under default-permissive evaluation. This state is intended only as a bootstrap until Senate publishes substantive rules. Prolonged absence of rules means no machine-actionable constraints exist, neutralizing Nomos's second compliance gate and allowing any action proposal to pass.

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "description": "Bill of Rights is a scaffold awaiting human authorship. The MSP requires a human-authored BoR to define the enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, and amendment protocol that the operational constitution must enforce. Without it, the DIO operates with an undefined security perimeter and no authoritative foundation for Nomos evaluation.",
      "msp_reference": "MSP Purpose: 'translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.' BoR scaffold explicitly shows version 1.0.0-seed with status 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship.'",
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "IMMEDIATE",
      "impact": "ENTERPRISE-WIDE",
      "priority_score": 100
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-GOV-001",
      "description": "Governance organs (Senate, Arbiter, Nomos) are unstable, flipping between DEGRADED and ALIVE every 30 seconds, as shown in Spine state transitions. The MSP amendment pipeline relies on Senate-Legislator responding to PEMs, but an unstable Senate cannot reliably process policy exceptions or amend the MSP. Nomos enforcement and Arbiter scope checks may be intermittent.",
      "msp_reference": "MSP Amendment Protocol: 'Amendments to this document originate only from Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos (#200) when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints. Senate-Legislator drafts the amendment...' Requires operational Senate, Nomos, and Arbiter.",
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "GOVERNANCE",
      "priority_score": 90
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-MSP-001",
      "description": "Mission Statement Protocol contains no rules; the organism operates under default-permissive evaluation. This state is intended only as a bootstrap until Senate publishes substantive rules. Prolonged absence of rules means no machine-actionable constraints exist, neutralizing Nomos's second compliance gate and allowing any action proposal to pass.",
      "msp_reference": "MSP Rules section: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules...'",
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "MODERATE",
      "impact": "OPERATIONAL SECURITY",
      "priority_score": 60
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-ARTICLE-I-UNDEFINED",
      "article_ref": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "description": "The scope of enterprise is entirely undefined. This means the Arbiter (#190) cannot enforce any perimeter, and the DIO has no declared boundary between legitimate and illegitimate action. All potential actions currently fall into an unconstrained space.",
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact": 10,
      "rationale": "Without a defined scope, the BoR provides no identity constraint. The recent goal-stream is empty, so no immediate conflict exists, but the absence itself is a constitutional void. As soon as any goal is dispatched it will operate without enterprise limits, risking unpredictable behavior."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-ARTICLE-II-UNDEFINED",
      "article_ref": "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities",
      "description": "Prohibited activities are entirely undefined. There are no 'never do' rules encoded in the constitution. The MSP is subordinate to the BoR, so no machine constraint can substitute for this missing article.",
      "criticality": 10,
      "urgency": 10,
      "impact": 10,
      "rationale": "Prohibitions are the primary negative identity of the organism. Their absence means that even with an empty goal-stream, the system lacks any invariant that would block a catastrophic action proposal. This is the most critical gap for safety."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-ARTICLE-III-UNDEFINED",
      "article_ref": "BoR Article III — Core Identity",
      "description": "Core identity is entirely undefined. The DIO has no stated mission, purpose, or defining characteristics that Arbiter can hash-check against scope queries.",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 9,
      "impact": 9,
      "rationale": "Core identity anchors all other articles. Without it, the organism cannot self-identify, leading to ambiguous interpretation of any future rules. It is slightly less urgent than prohibitions only because prohibitions directly block harm, but identity is foundational."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-ARTICLE-IV-UNDEFINED",
      "article_ref": "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation",
      "description": "The reservation of human authority is undefined. There is no BoR-level declaration of what decisions remain exclusively human, leaving the principal's sovereignty unstated.",
      "criticality": 9,
      "urgency": 8,
      "impact": 10,
      "rationale": "If a goal conflicts with human authority (e.g., self-amendment), there is no BoR article to block it. In the absence of recent goals, this remains latent but is a high-impact gap for governance integrity."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-ARTICLE-V-UNDEFINED",
      "article_ref": "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol",
      "description": "The BoR amendment protocol is undefined. The only protection is the 'human-principal-only' authority line in the preamble, but there is no article spelling out the secure process.",
      "criticality": 8,
      "urgency": 7,
      "impact": 8,
      "rationale": "While the MSP acknowledges BoR amendments are human-only, the BoR itself is silent. This gap could lead to procedural ambiguity if an amendment is attempted. It is less urgent than the identity and prohibition gaps."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-ARTICLE-VI-UNDEFINED",
      "article_ref": "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules",
      "description": "Interpretation rules are undefined. There is no instruction on how to resolve conflicts between articles or how to handle unforeseen scenarios.",
      "criticality": 7,
      "urgency": 6,
      "impact": 7,
      "rationale": "Interpretation becomes critical only after other articles are populated. Its current absence is a secondary gap, but still a constitutional void that could enable ambiguous adjudication later."
    }
  ]
}
```

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Undefined Bill of Rights – All Articles Are Scaffold",
      "description": "The entire BoR (Articles I-VI) is pending human authorship. Without defined enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, and interpretation rules, the Arbiter (#190) has no substantive perimeter to enforce at scope gate. Nomos (#200) cannot adjudicate BoR compliance for any Action Proposal. Every proposed action would pass the BoR gate vacuously, leaving the system effectively ungoverned and exposed to unbounded operation.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I, II, III, IV, V, VI (all scaffold)",
      "criticality": "Critical",
      "urgency": "Immediate",
      "impact": "Complete absence of safety boundaries; system operates without constitutional limits",
      "priority": "P0"
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Empty MSP Ruleset – Default-Permissive Operational Mode",
      "description": "The MSP (v1.0.0-seed) contains no rules. Under default-permissive evaluation, any Action Proposal passes the second compliance gate without constraint. Combined with the missing BoR content, the entire constitutional framework is inert. Nomos cannot enforce operational invariants or risk-tier constraints; the DIO lacks any machine-actionable compliance objectives.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules section ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation...')",
      "criticality": "Critical",
      "urgency": "Immediate",
      "impact": "No operational guardrails; all APs execute unconditionally",
      "priority": "P0"
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "gap_summary": "Bill of Rights entirely scaffold; no relational principles for person-attention defined.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section": "All articles, especially Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) and Article I (Scope of Enterprise)",
        "detail": "The BoR is a scaffold awaiting human authorship. It lacks any clause that mandates the system to respect, maintain, and adapt to the human principal's attentional availability, communication preferences, or relational well-being."
      },
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority_score": 27,
      "attention_needed": "Constitutional-level requirement for the system to perpetually attend to Leon's personhood — his current cognitive load, desire for interaction, and preferred communication modalities — must be defined.",
      "rationale": "Without explicit BoR enshrinement, the DIO's highest law provides no safeguard that the system will avoid overwhelming, ignoring, or misattuning to the human. All subsequent MSP rules would lack a constitutional mandate to enforce person-attention, leaving the agency free to deprioritize Leon's relational state."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "gap_summary": "MSP Rules are empty; no machine-actionable person-attention constraints for Nomos evaluation.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Rules (currently '(none)') and Purpose",
        "detail": "The MSP's operational constitution contains zero rules. The Purpose states it translates BoR into machine-actionable objectives, invariants, and constraints. Since BoR lacks relational principles, even if added, there is currently no rule set that would cause Nomos to reject an Action Proposal that disregards Leon's attentional needs."
      },
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority_score": 27,
      "attention_needed": "Rules that encode person-attention checks, such as requiring a current attentional state estimate before initiating contact, or preventing high-frequency interruptions without consent, must be published under MSP.",
      "rationale": "Nomos's compliance gate currently default-permissive; without person-attention rules, any automated or Senator-proposed action can interact with Leon regardless of his state, directly undermining human authority and trust."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "gap_summary": "Minder/Hippocampus lacks observations of Leon's relational state and conversational context.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Minder/Hippocampus slices (data) and the implicit requirement of BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) and MSP Purpose",
        "section": "recent_observations, hippocampus.recent_conversations",
        "detail": "The existing observations are exclusively technical deductions about Leon's architectural preferences. There are no entries about his current mood, attentional bandwidth, communication frustration, or desire for brevity. The hippocampus recent conversations list is empty, providing no short-term memory of recent exchanges to adapt responses."
      },
      "criticality": "High",
      "urgency": "High",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority_score": 27,
      "attention_needed": "The system must capture person-specific relational signals — such as indicators of Leon being tired, busy, or preferring minimal interaction — and maintain a rolling summary of recent dialogues to avoid redundant queries and to calibrate tone and depth.",
      "rationale": "Without this data, the DIO is blind to Leon's real-time person-attention needs. It cannot decide when to escalate, remain silent, or condense information. This blindness threatens the practical realization of any human authority reservation."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "gap_summary": "MSP Amendment Protocol's HOM notification lacks attention-aware delivery constraints.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Amendment Protocol",
        "detail": "The protocol states 'an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox.' It does not include any rule about querying Leon's present attentional state, batching notifications, or respecting do-not-disturb periods before emitting a potentially intrusive alert."
      },
      "criticality": "Medium",
      "urgency": "Medium",
      "impact": "High",
      "priority_score": 12,
      "attention_needed": "The HOM notification pipeline must be conditioned on person-attention checks — e.g., confirming Leon is receptive, or buffering low-priority notifications — to avoid disrupting his workflow with every staked review request.",
      "rationale": "Amendment reviews inherently require human attention. If the notification mechanism ignores Leon's state, the system risks annoying interruptions that degrade trust and could cause him to delay governance decisions, undermining the DIO's operational cadence."
    }
  ]
}
